title: Screen Time Rules for Little Kids
summary: The government says kids under five should not watch screens for more than one hour each day. Kids under two should not watch screens alone. Parents should watch screens with their kids and choose slow videos. They can swap screen time for reading books or playing games together. This helps kids sleep better and play more, which is important for growing up. Many kids watch screens every day by age two, but it's better when adults join in. Parents should also show good habits by not using screens too much themselves. The rules might change as we learn more.

questions.question: How much screen time should kids under five have each day?
Should kids under two watch screens alone?
What should parents do instead of screen time?
Why is it good for kids to sleep and play more?
Who makes the screen time rules?
What should parents show by their own screen use?

background_read: Screen time means using devices like TVs, tablets, and phones. For young children, too much screen time can affect their sleep and how much they move and play, which are important for healthy development. The government gives advice to help parents make good choices, like limiting screen use and doing activities together instead. This guidance is based on what experts know so far, and it might be updated as more information becomes available.
Article_Structure: WHO: The government and experts like Dame Rachel de Souza and Professor Russell Viner.
WHAT: New rules say kids under five should have no more than one hour of screen time daily, and under-twos should not watch alone.
WHERE: This applies in England, as part of government guidance.
WHY: To help kids sleep better, be more active, and develop well, since too much solo screen time can be harmful.

perspectives.description: Parents like Alexis find it hard to reduce screen time because it helps with daily tasks and avoids conflicts, but they want to cut down for their kids' health.
